title: Hlabisa hosts strategic meeting in Nelson Mandela Bay
description: EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The Minister of...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The Minister of Cooperative Governance and Traditional Affairs (CoGTA), Velenkosini Hlabisa, has held a high-level strategic meeting with civil society and key stakeholders in the Nelson Mandela Bay Metro in the Eastern Cape.","html":"EASTERN CAPE NEWS - The Minister of Cooperative Governance and Traditional Affairs (CoGTA), Velenkosini Hlabisa, has held a high-level strategic meeting with civil society and key stakeholders in the Nelson Mandela Bay Metro in the Eastern Cape."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The gathering that was held at the weekend followed the department’s appointment of a 10-member team, in accordance with Section 154(1) of the Constitution, to assist the city council in improving governance and facilitating service delivery."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The engagement brought together representatives from the National CoGTA, the Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality, Eastern Cape CoGTA, the Nelson Mandela Bay Business Chamber, civil society groups, and other important stakeholders.","html":"The engagement brought together representatives from the National CoGTA, the Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality, Eastern Cape CoGTA, the Nelson Mandela Bay Business Chamber, civil society groups, and other important stakeholders."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The civil society coalition emphasised that it is non-partisan and represents the interests of the community, while participants highlighted several critical issues, including the ongoing instability in municipal leadership, persistent shortcomings in service delivery, and the deterioration of essential infrastructure.","html":"The civil society coalition emphasised that it is non-partisan and represents the interests of the community, while participants highlighted several critical issues, including the ongoing instability in municipal leadership, persistent shortcomings in service delivery, and the deterioration of essential infrastructure."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"They also expressed concern about the perceived neglect of young people’s voices in important development decisions, rising crime rates, unsafe road conditions, and challenges related to water supply and sanitation systems.","html":"They also expressed concern about the perceived neglect of young people’s voices in important development decisions, rising crime rates, unsafe road conditions, and challenges related to water supply and sanitation systems."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In addition, there were worries about the electricity supply and associated issues.","html":"In addition, there were worries about the electricity supply and associated issues."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The coalition, along with the Business Chamber, stressed the importance of transparent governance, operational efficiency, and strengthened partnerships to restore investor confidence, protect manufacturing industries, and drive economic growth,” the statement read.","html":"“The coalition, along with the Business Chamber, stressed the importance of transparent governance, operational efficiency, and strengthened partnerships to restore investor confidence, protect manufacturing industries, and drive economic growth,” the statement read."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"According to the department, the faith-based representatives highlighted the link between economic challenges, poverty, and inequality, calling for greater collaboration, partnerships, and community-focused solutions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Transparency, inclusivity in oversight, and stakeholder engagement were also prioritised during discussions.","html":"Transparency, inclusivity in oversight, and stakeholder engagement were also prioritised during discussions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Hlabisa stressed the importance of merit-based appointments, stating that “positions in government must be filled by people with the right qualifications and competencies. Cadre deployment must be done away with to ensure effective service delivery and good governance.”","html":"Hlabisa stressed the importance of merit-based appointments, stating that “positions in government must be filled by people with the right qualifications and competencies. Cadre deployment must be done away with to ensure effective service delivery and good governance.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Mayor Babalwa Lobishe highlighted initiatives already underway to strengthen the municipality, including filling key vacant positions and improving systems and structures to enhance service delivery.","html":"Mayor Babalwa Lobishe highlighted initiatives already underway to strengthen the municipality, including filling key vacant positions and improving systems and structures to enhance service delivery."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"She also announced the facilitation of a Youth Consultative Forum to engage young people in the development agenda.","html":"She also announced the facilitation of a Youth Consultative Forum to engage young people in the development agenda."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“The mayor committed to ongoing engagement with all stakeholders and emphasised consequence management for officials implicated in wrongdoing.”","html":"“The mayor committed to ongoing engagement with all stakeholders and emphasised consequence management for officials implicated in wrongdoing.”"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"According to the department, the Section 154 support, which deployed 10 experts, will diagnose challenges, identify gaps, and enhance municipal capacity.","html":"According to the department, the Section 154 support, which deployed 10 experts, will diagnose challenges, identify gaps, and enhance municipal capacity."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Minister explained the difference between Section 139 and Section 154 interventions, noting the importance of timely support to ensure the municipality meets its objectives.","html":"The Minister explained the difference between Section 139 and Section 154 interventions, noting the importance of timely support to ensure the municipality meets its objectives."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Hlabisa also stressed the urgent social issues facing the metro, including crime, gender-based violence and femicide (GBVF), as well as the pervasive effects of alcohol, drugs, and substance abuse."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"“Crime, GBVF, and substance abuse pose serious threats to the safety and well-being of our communities. We cannot separate social challenges from economic and governance issues. It is essential for government, civil society, religious institutions, and communities to work together to protect our people, especially women and children and to build safer, healthier communities,” he said.","html":"“Crime, GBVF, and substance abuse pose serious threats to the safety and well-being of our communities. We cannot separate social challenges from economic and governance issues. It is essential for government, civil society, religious institutions, and communities to work together to protect our people, especially women and children and to build safer, healthier communities,” he said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Mayor is expected to meet with all stakeholders in early 2026 to discuss a plan of action aimed at revitalising the Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality and promoting sustainable development for the community.","html":"The Mayor is expected to meet with all stakeholders in early 2026 to discuss a plan of action aimed at revitalising the Nelson Mandela Bay Municipality and promoting sustainable development for the community."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"In closing, the Minister reaffirmed government’s commitment to working with the people of Nelson Mandela Bay to build a stable, inclusive, and thriving municipality. – SAnews.gov.za","html":"In closing, the Minister reaffirmed government’s commitment to working with the people of Nelson Mandela Bay to build a stable, inclusive, and thriving municipality. – SAnews.gov.za"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Hlabisa hosts strategic meeting in Nelson Mandela Bay
